sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 14

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 14:1
items.html: (to the question) Three degrees: the lower one, the upper one, and the upper-upper. And by the word "upper one" the meaning is that degree which stands in Mochin of Yenika [suckling], which is fit to rise as MAN [Mayin Nukvin] of itself for the sake of the Mochin of Gadlut. And it necessarily takes together with it the GE [Galgalta ve Eynaim] of the lower one, which cleave to its AHP [Awzen, Hotem, Peh] of Gadlut. Thus there are three degrees in every raising of MAN.
